one_time_description: Disposal/write-off of investment in Nakoda Developments Corp. (GBE dissolved March 27, 2024), resulting in a loss of $531,656 recorded in revenue
economic_notes: Nation holds 8.3% interest in Keseechiwan Holdings LP and FHQTC Developments LP. Operates Nakota Junction Convenience Store Inc. Member of File Hills Qu'Appelle Tribal Council (11-nation organization). Nakoda Developments Corp. dissolved March 27, 2024. Ongoing settlement claims related to 1905 Surrender, Cypress Hills, Treaty 4 Agricultural Benefits, Misadministration Litigation.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED on two bases: (1) Nakoda Developments Corp. not consolidated due to insufficient audit evidence despite being controlled by the Nation (dissolved March 27, 2024); (2) changes in loan balances related to settlement claims classified as professional fees could not be verified. Province is Saskatchewan (auditor based in Saskatoon, SK; Nation is Carry the Kettle which is located in SK). Consolidated entities include Carry the Kettle Nakoda Nation government, CMHC Housing Fund, and Nakota Junction Convenience Store Inc. Modified equity entities include Nakoda Developments Corporation (dissolved), Keseechiwan Holdings LP, and FHQTC Developments LP. total_government_transfers calculated as ISC $21,946,524 + Ottawa Trust $1,326,805 + First Nations Trust $1,604,511 + CMHC $539,823 + FHQTC $233,158 + Living Sky $180,345 + Canadian Heritage $5,577 = $25,836,743. annual_debt_service is repayment of long term debt $809,492 + repayment of capital lease $71,994 = $881,486.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
